header.title: Resume
header.summary: A concise view of my experience, education, and projects — aligned with what you'll find across this site.

contact.name: Nergiz Rahimzade
contact.title: Computer Science Student · AI & Web Builder

bullets: Develop responsive user interfaces using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Collaborate with developers to implement UI components and improve usability.
Apply best practices for clean, maintainable front-end code.
Transferred here after completing a year of university education in Turkey.

projects.name: Tower Defense Game
bullets: Developed a tower defense game in a team of three using Java and JavaFX.
Implemented enemy movement, tower placement, and game logic.

projects.name: MUI Practice Website
bullets: Built a website to practice using Material UI components.
Customized themes and responsive layouts using MUI.

projects.name: Task Management Website
bullets: Built a task management web application with React and TypeScript.
Implemented component-based architecture and state management.

projects.name: Distance Calculator Website
bullets: Created a web application that calculates distance and travel duration between two cities.
Integrated several external APIs to fetch real-time distance and duration data as well as weather, emergency contact number, local date & time, list of activities to do in the destination city.

projects.name: Check&Chef Pantry
bullets: Built this website with teammates in a 24 hour hackathon — SacHacks.
Implemented the code for our website; we didn't use any backend and a collection of data was provided to us via Google Sheets.
Used an external API to get the data and used React, MUI for the frontend.

projects.name: TransferBuddy AI
bullets: TransferBuddy is an AI application for California community college students to help with their transfer process.
Built this for HackDavis with teammates; I implemented AI integration, Express JS backend, and testing AI.

The transfer process refers to community college students transferring to a 4-year university for their Bachelor's degree. We plan to take this project further and ship it as a mobile application by August 2026.

projects.name: Smart Cane
bullets: SmartCane is created for visually impaired people to help with their everyday lives.
Built this for HackStorm with a teammate; I implemented computer vision using the llama-4-scout model via Groq API, text-to-speech using Microsoft Embedded TTS, and the overall software structure.
SmartCane warns the user about everything they need to be aware of — from elevator arrivals and steep edges/staircases to crowded places and available seats relative to the user.
